steps.title: Extract data points from the table
steps.content: From the table: when $c = 3$, $p = 9$; when $c = 5$, $p = 11$; when $c = 10$, $p = 16$.

steps.title: Calculate the slope
steps.content:
Using any two points, say $(3, 9)$ and $(5, 11)$:
$$m = \frac{11 - 9}{5 - 3} = \frac{2}{2} = 1$$
Verify with the third point: from $(5, 11)$ to $(10, 16)$ gives slope $\frac{16 - 11}{10 - 5} = \frac{5}{5} = 1$ ✓

steps.title: Write the equation using point-slope form
steps.content:
Using point $(3, 9)$ and slope $m = 1$:
$$p - 9 = 1(c - 3)$$
$$p - 9 = c - 3$$
$$p = c + 6$$
Rearranging:
$$c - p = -6$$
That matches choice A — A is correct.
